title: Poa glabrescens
intro: Poa glabrescens, also known as smooth meadow-grass, is a species of perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Poa glabrescens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Poa glabrescens is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is used to support respiratory health, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Poa glabrescens is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Poa, which consists of many species of grasses. Within the genus Poa, Poa glabrescens is classified as a member of the subgenus Homalocaulon. Poa glabrescens is native to the mountainous regions of China. The classification of Poa glabrescens is further detailed in botanical classification systems, such as the APG IV system.
